futures_this_morning: S&P 500 futures near flat (-0.16%); Nasdaq 100 -0.3%; Dow futures +94 pts; S&P 500 up ~+0.8% for the week
events: Q2 bank earnings start week of Jul 14: Citi/WFC/GS/MS Tue Jul 14; JPMorgan Wed Jul 15
Big Tech earnings (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, META, AMZN) expected late July
FOMC meeting Jul 28-29; rate decision announced Wed 2026-07-29 2:00pm ET
rates: steady — Fed funds 3.50-3.75%, held for 4 consecutive meetings; next decision Jul 29
geopolitical: Active US-Iran military conflict: US struck 80+ Iranian targets Jul 7 after Iran attacked US military bases in Gulf states; Iran retaliated vs US forces in Bahrain and Kuwait; Brent crude +5.4% to $78.19; IMF cut global 2026 growth forecast to 3.0% from 3.5%; ceasefire declared then rescinded by Trump
jobs: June 2026: +57K payrolls (vs +113K expected, roughly half); unemployment 4.2%; April and May revised down 74K combined; wage growth +3.5% YoY — labor market clearly weakening
valuation: Trailing P/E ~25x (85th percentile historically, rated Expensive); forward P/E ~20x (at 5-year average); CAPE signals historically overvalued; Q2 2026 earnings growth forecast +22% (strong)
note: Posture CAUTIOUS. Four concurrent headwinds: (1) active US-Iran military conflict with oil spike and global growth downgrade; (2) CPI print due in 4 days — market-moving event under inflation uncertainty; (3) major bank earnings start next week, first real Q2 data; (4) trailing P/E expensive at 85th percentile historically. The jobs miss (+57K vs +113K) adds an economic softening signal. SPY is above its 200-day average (+8.3%) so the trend is still up, but the risk-reward for deploying fresh cash this week is poor. All-cash account — no urgency. Demand wider margin of safety than normal before any buy.
summary: Cautious posture. SPY in uptrend but expensive by historical trailing P/E. Active US-Iran conflict has spiked oil and cut global growth forecasts. CPI report is 4 days away; major bank earnings start next week. June jobs were half of expectations. No rush to deploy cash — wait for margin of safety.
risks: US-Iran military escalation could spike oil further and hit equities broadly
CPI Jul 14 could surprise hot, delaying Fed cuts and pressuring valuations
Weak jobs trend may signal recession risk if confirmed in future reports
Bank earnings may reveal credit stress or lower guidance given macro uncertainty
FOMC Jul 29 — if inflation remains sticky, no cut expected and tone could turn hawkish
